title: Self-Governed Tiny House Village
description: A democratically self-governed tiny-house village in NE Portland sheltering up to 60 people. Residents run the community together under a shared agreement. Entry is through the Village Intake Committee, and there is nearly always a waitlist.
engage: Call 503-281-1604 to ask about the intake process or to schedule a tour. Review the Village Intake Committee process at dignityvillage.org.
content:
About

Dignity Village pioneered the self-governed "Village Model" of shelter — a democratic community where residents share responsibility for running the village. It houses up to 60 people nightly.

Who It's For

Adults experiencing homelessness seeking shelter and community.

How to Get In

  • Go through the Village Intake Committee process
  • There is nearly always a waitlist for year-round spots
  • Residents must follow the Entrance Agreement and community manual

Location

9401 NE Sunderland Ave, Portland.

title: Job Placement for Job Seekers
description: A staffing agency that connects job seekers with temporary, government, and direct-hire positions at no cost to the job seeker. Browse open jobs or join their talent community with a general application.
engage: Browse and apply to open positions at dpistaffing.com/for-job-seekers/jobs, or submit a general application. Call 800-518-6637 or email info@dpistaffing.com.
content:
Services

DPI Staffing places job seekers into temporary, government, and direct-hire roles across multiple industries and states (including Oregon and Washington). If there's no immediate match, you can join their talent community for future openings.

Cost

Free for job seekers.

How to Apply

Browse current openings online or submit a general application. Portland and Salem offices serve Oregon.

title: Family Shelter & Homelessness Prevention
description: Shelter, prevention, and stabilization services for families with children experiencing or at risk of homelessness in inner NE and SE Portland. Operates a rotating shelter with partner congregations plus case management to help families regain stable housing.
engage: Call 503-753-3960 or email info@pdxfp.org to ask about family shelter and prevention services. You can also call 211 for real-time family shelter openings.
content:
Services

  • Emergency shelter for families with children (rotating congregation model)
  • Homelessness prevention and rent/stability support
  • Case management and housing stabilization

Who It's For

Families with children experiencing or at risk of homelessness in NE and SE Portland.

How to Access

Contact Family Promise of Metro East at 503-753-3960 or info@pdxfp.org. (Other Family Promise affiliates serve Washington County and Gresham.)

title: Detox (Withdrawal Management)
description: Medically-supervised withdrawal management to safely begin recovery from alcohol and other drugs. Access to treatment is not denied due to inability to pay; sliding-scale programs available.
engage: Call 503-535-1151 or use the walk-in / same-day care process at forahealth.org/walk-in-process to get started.
content:
Program

Medically-supervised detox with clinical monitoring and support as you withdraw from substances, providing a safe start to recovery and a bridge into further treatment.

Cost

Treatment is not denied due to inability to pay. Sliding-scale programs available; insurance accepted.

title: Residential Treatment
description: Inpatient residential addiction treatment providing 24-hour structured care, counseling, and support — including a medically-monitored option for higher-acuity needs.
engage: Complete the residential interest form at forahealth.org/adults-get-started or call 503-535-1151.
content:
Program

Live-in treatment in a structured therapeutic setting with individual and group counseling, medical support, and case management. A medically-monitored level is available for those who need closer clinical oversight.

Cost

Sliding scale available; treatment not denied for inability to pay.

title: Outpatient Treatment
description: Community-based outpatient addiction treatment for people who need support while keeping up with work, school, and family. Counseling and groups on a flexible schedule.
engage: Call 503-535-1151 or use the walk-in / same-day care process to begin. Insurance verification at forahealth.org/verify-insurance.
content:
Program

Outpatient counseling and group treatment that lets you live at home and maintain daily responsibilities while in recovery.

Cost

Sliding scale; treatment not denied for inability to pay.

title: Medications for Addiction Treatment (MAT)
description: Medication-assisted treatment (such as buprenorphine and other medications) combined with counseling to support recovery from opioid and alcohol use disorders.
engage: Call 503-535-1151 or use the walk-in process at forahealth.org/walk-in-process.
content:
Program

Medications for addiction treatment paired with clinical and counseling support to reduce cravings and support long-term recovery.

Cost

Sliding scale; treatment not denied for inability to pay.

title: DUII Program
description: Specialized treatment and education program for people with DUII-related offenses, meeting court and diversion requirements.
engage: Call 503-535-1151 to enroll in the DUII program.
content:
Program

Assessment, education, and treatment for individuals with DUII charges or convictions, structured to meet Oregon court and diversion requirements.

title: Peer Recovery Mentors
description: Peer-led recovery support from mentors with lived experience, walking alongside clients through treatment and early recovery.
engage: Ask your Fora Health care team about connecting with a peer recovery mentor, or call 503-535-1151.
content:
Program

Certified peer mentors with lived recovery experience provide encouragement, connection, and practical support throughout treatment and beyond.

title: Wraparound Services for Abuse Survivors
description: A family justice center in Washington County bringing 20+ partner agencies under one roof so survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, child abuse, elder abuse, and exploitation can get help without retelling their trauma. Free, all ages, any language.
engage: Call (503) 430-8300 (24/7) or walk in Monday–Friday 8:30am–4:00pm at 1100 NE Compton Dr, Hillsboro. All services are free.
content:
Services Under One Roof

  • Intervention: child abuse support and legal assistance
  • Prevention: social and financial support to reduce risk
  • Healing: mental health counseling and trauma support
  • Hope: arts, community engagement, and leadership programs

Who It's For

Survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, child abuse, elder abuse, and exploitation in Washington County — children, youth, adults, and elders.

Cost & Access

All services are free and offered in any language. Call 24/7 or walk in during business hours.

title: Family Shelter & Housing Stability
description: An emergency family shelter in Tigard providing shelter plus support services to help families with children experiencing homelessness reach lasting housing stability. About half of those served are children.
engage: Call (503) 443-6084 or visit gncnw.org's Get Help section to learn about shelter and intake.
content:
Services

Residential shelter for families combined with support services (case management, resources, and stability planning) to help families secure and keep long-term housing.

Who It's For

Families with children experiencing housing insecurity or homelessness.

Location

11130 SW Greenburg Rd, Tigard.

title: Emergency Shelter (Referral-Based)
description: Low-barrier emergency shelter for up to 150 men, women, and families experiencing homelessness, with safe beds, food, and a work program. Referral-only, with same-day placement available 7 days a week, 8am–8pm.
engage: Ask a referral partner to refer you, or call the Hope Center at (971) 333-5070 for help finding a referral partner. Same-day placement 7 days a week, 8am–8pm.
content:
Services

  • Safe emergency shelter (up to 150 people)
  • Meals
  • Work program
  • Path into the optional Reentry Program

Who It's For

Men, women, and families experiencing homelessness.

How to Get In

Referral-only. Referral partners can arrange same-day placement 7 days a week, 8am–8pm. Call (971) 333-5070 if you need help finding a referral partner.

title: Reentry Program
description: An optional high-barrier reentry track at Bybee Lakes for shelter guests ready for deeper support — case management, skill-building classes, job search help, financial coaching, ID services, health care navigation, and recovery support.
engage: Enter through the Bybee Lakes emergency shelter, then work with staff to join the Reentry Program. Call (971) 333-5070 for details.
content:
What's Included

  • Case management staff
  • Skill-building classes
  • Job search assistance
  • Financial coaching and planning
  • Identification services
  • Mental and physical health care navigation
  • Recovery support meetings (as appropriate)

Who It's For

Shelter guests ready to work toward stability and self-sufficiency.

title: Peer Support & Community Meetings
description: A peer-led "community of belonging" offering talking circles, in-reach/outreach, and weekly Friday Night Community Meetings (hybrid in-person and Zoom) for people in transition and recovery.
engage: Join the Friday Night Community Meeting (5:30–6:30pm PST, in person or via Zoom), or call 503-344-6710. Details at irontribenetwork.org.
content:
Peer Support

  • Talking circles and community gatherings
  • In-reach and outreach programs
  • Friday Night Community Meetings — 5:30–6:30pm PST, hybrid (in person + Zoom)
  • Family reunification support

Who It's For

Individuals and families overcoming barriers while transitioning to a life that reflects their values, including people in recovery.

title: Recovery Housing
description: Clean and sober recovery housing, including ODHS program housing and OHA Behavioral Health Resource Network (BHRN) housing across multiple Oregon counties (Clatsop, Columbia, Marion, Multnomah).
engage: Call 503-344-6710 or email through irontribenetwork.org to ask about recovery housing availability.
content:
Housing

  • Clean & sober recovery housing
  • ODHS program housing
  • OHA BHRN housing in Clatsop, Columbia, Marion, and Multnomah counties

Who It's For

People in recovery seeking a stable, substance-free place to live.

title: Access Center (Homeless Youth Entry Point)
description: The single point of entry for homeless youth ages 14–24 seeking services through Portland's Homeless Youth Continuum — engagement, intake, screening, and referrals to shelter and support.
engage: Contact the Access Center at 503-432-3986 or visit janusyouth.org/find-help to get connected to youth services.
content:
What It Does

The Access Center is where homeless and unstably-housed youth (ages 14–24) start — staff provide engagement, intake, screening, and referrals into shelter, housing, and community services.

Who It's For

Youth ages 14–24 experiencing homelessness in Multnomah County.

title: Porch Light Crisis Shelter
description: An overnight crisis shelter for youth ready to work toward ending their homelessness — nourishing meals, bunk beds, showers, and hygiene amenities. Open every night 8:45pm–8:45am. All services are LGBTQ affirmative.
engage: Connect through the Janus Access Center (503-432-3986). Porch Light is open every night, 8:45pm–8:45am.
content:
Services

  • Safe overnight shelter, every night of the year (8:45pm–8:45am)
  • Nourishing meals
  • Bunk beds, showers, and hygiene amenities

Who It's For

Youth working toward long-term solutions to their homelessness. All services are LGBTQ affirmative.

title: Street Light Shelter
description: A safe, youth-friendly overnight shelter open every night of the year for young people ages 16–24 experiencing homelessness in Multnomah County.
engage: Connect through the Janus Access Center (503-432-3986) or Yellow Brick Road street outreach.
content:
Services

Safe, comfortable, youth-friendly shelter every night of the year for youth ages 16–24 experiencing homelessness.

Who It's For

Youth ages 16–24 in Multnomah County.

title: Yellow Brick Road Street Outreach
description: One of the longest-running youth street outreach programs in the Pacific Northwest (40+ years). Outreach specialists, peer mentors, and volunteers engage young people on the streets of Portland every night, offering supplies, connection, and links to services.
engage: Yellow Brick Road outreach teams are on the streets nightly. Reach Janus at 503-432-3986 for more.
content:
What It Does

Professional outreach specialists — joined by peer mentors and community volunteers — go out every night of the year to meet homeless and street-involved youth where they are, providing basic supplies, connection, and pathways to shelter and services.

Who It's For

Street-involved and homeless youth in Portland.

title: Abuelos Transitional Recovery Housing
description: Culturally- and linguistically-specific transitional recovery housing for the Latino/a/x/e community, centering familia and community bonds. Part of Juntos NW's work expanding behavioral health access for Latino and Indigenous communities in Oregon.
engage: Join the Abuelos housing waitlist at 503-449-9126 or 971-500-2064, or email juntos@juntonw.com. Office at 3516 NE 71st Ave, Portland, Mon–Fri 8am–5pm.
content:
Program

Abuelos provides transitional recovery housing designed by and for the Latino community — culturally and linguistically specific, emphasizing familia and community as foundations of recovery.

Who It's For

Latino/a/x/e community members in recovery.

About Juntos NW

Juntos NW expands access to behavioral health for Latino/a/x/e and Indigenous communities through outreach, education, and collective action, with an emphasis on language access and cultural celebration.

How to Access

Call the Abuelos waitlist line at 503-449-9126 or 971-500-2064.
centerings: People in Recovery; Spanish Speakers; BIPOC
